page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7nex.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical Intel Xeon 8369B cores running at 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and network interfaces with a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a moderate single-core capability but limited multi-core scaling. The stress-ng single-core CPU test scores 1882.09, placing it in the average tier, while the multi-core score of 3860.98 falls into the weak category. Memory bandwidth benchmarks are also relatively low, with read-only large block speeds at 30.0 GB/sec and write speeds at 25.02 GB/sec. Single-threaded operations like gzip compression (27.75 GB/sec) and bzip3 decompression (10.49 GB/sec) perform at average levels, but multi-threaded bzip3 decompression drops to a poor rating at 20.24 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.g7nex.xlarge offers dedicated Intel Xeon resources with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a stable option for workloads that require predictable CPU allocation without burstable limitations. However, the lack of bundled local storage and the weak multi-core and database benchmark results (such as 2009.27 in Passmark database operations and 3.91 million operations/sec in Redis SET) present clear tradeoffs. This instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads, light web serving, single-threaded processing tasks, and development environments that do not demand high memory throughput or parallelized compute scaling.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7nex.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing a 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data reveals average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1882.09, but highlights tradeoffs in multi-threaded scaling and memory bandwidth, where large-block read speeds reach 30.0 GB/sec. Database operations and multi-core benchmarks fall into the weaker tiers, indicating that this instance is best suited for general-purpose applications, light web serving, and single-threaded processing tasks rather than high-performance database or parallel computing workloads.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7nex.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, offering stable single-core performance with moderate multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7nex.xlarge features 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, delivering balanced general-purpose compute on KVM.
tagline: A dedicated x86 virtual server offering balanced compute and memory resources for predictable, single-threaded and general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B physical cores at 2.7 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage included
Average single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds
Suited for general-purpose applications, light web serving, and development tasks
